channel_1.text: Heads up everyone — the Marriott has confirmed our room block! Book using code FBLA2026 for the discounted rate. Deadline is March 28.
The conference schedule just dropped! Check-in starts at 8 AM tomorrow. Opening ceremony is at 7 PM in the Grand Ballroom. Business casual for Day 1, professional attire for Day 2.
I just finished packing. Three copies of my materials, laptop charged, backup on USB drive. Not taking any chances! 😂
Is anyone driving up together from Cherry Creek? I have room for 2 more people. Leaving at 7 AM Friday.
I'll take a spot! Messaging you now.
Reminder from Ms. Rivera: all students need to submit the signed permission slip to room 204 by TODAY. Anyone who hasn't submitted cannot attend. This is mandatory.
This is my third State Conference and I'm still nervous 😅 The energy at Marriott DTC is electric. You're all going to crush it.
Opening ceremony was AMAZING. Keynote speaker was incredible — really got me fired up. Who's going to the networking dinner tonight?
The keynote was so good. 'Leadership isn't a title, it's a choice' — writing that down forever.
Good night everyone. Presentations start at 8 AM. Set two alarms. See you at breakfast!
GOOD MORNING DISTRICT 12! It's competition day! We've been working for this all year. Go show those judges what Cherry Creek is made of! 🚀🚀🚀
Feeling ready! My presentation is at 10:30 AM in Room C-4. App is loaded and looking great. Let's GO! 💪
See everyone at the Marriott tomorrow! 🎉

channel_2.text: Hey everyone! Who's competing in Mobile App Dev? Let's share tips. I've done this event twice — happy to help first-timers.
First time here! Built my app in React Native with Expo. It's a full-featured FBLA member app — news feed, calendar, chat, resources. Really proud of how it turned out.
React Native is such a smart choice for FBLA — iOS AND Android from one codebase. Judges love seeing cross-platform apps. What state management are you using?
Zustand for state management, AsyncStorage for persistence. Clean MVVM architecture. I followed the scoring rubric closely — every feature maps to a criterion.
Pro tip: Judges ALWAYS ask about input validation. Make sure you can demo both syntactic AND semantic validation clearly. They specifically look for inline error messages, not alerts.
My app is built with Flutter. Different tech stack but same principles. Made sure to nail the architecture diagram in my planning docs.
Another tip: time yourself strictly. 7 minutes goes FAST. I recommend: 1 min intro, 4 min demo, 2 min architecture/planning. Don't let the demo run long.
What's everyone's app topic? I did a study planner with AI suggestions. Vedaant's doing FBLA member management which is brilliant for this competition.
Yeah — building an app FOR FBLA in an FBLA competition is very meta 😄 Hopefully judges see the creativity in it. The chat system is my wow factor.
That's genius. When the judge opens your app and it has a chat feature — with THIS conversation in it — that's going to blow their mind 🤯
Good luck to all my Mobile App Dev students tomorrow! I am so proud of what you've built. Go show them what Cherry Creek can do. 💙
Anyone else nervous for their presentation? 😅
Nervous but ready. Just ran through my demo one more time. Everything works perfectly. Let's do this!

channel_3.text: Website Design crew — who's going to share their site for peer feedback before State?
I'll share mine! It's a responsive e-commerce site. React frontend, clean CSS Grid layout. Accessibility score is 97 on Lighthouse.
97 Lighthouse score is incredible. The judges really pay attention to accessibility. What did you do for keyboard navigation?
Full keyboard trap for modals, skip-to-content link, proper focus management, ARIA labels on all icons. It passed WCAG 2.1 AA completely.
I focused heavily on the visual design this year. Custom color system, fluid typography, micro-animations. Hoping the design score carries me.
Just finished my portfolio site for the demo!

channel_4.text: District 12 family! State Conference is 2 days away. Let's go over the game plan one more time so everyone is prepared.
Bus departs Cherry Creek at 7:00 AM Friday (April 2). Be there at 6:45 AM. Late arrivals will not be able to board. Bring your FBLA card.
What's the dress code for Day 1 again?
Day 1 (Friday): Business casual. Day 2 (Saturday — competition day): Business professional. Day 3 (Sunday): Business professional for awards. When in doubt, overdress.
Who's excited about the business plan pitch? I've been working on mine for 6 months. This is it. 🏆
I'm nervous for Organizational Leadership but I know my material cold. Practice makes perfect — I've done my speech 40 times this week 😅
40 times! That's commitment. You've absolutely got this Amara.
Chapter photo op at 6:30 PM Friday in the hotel lobby before the opening ceremony! Everyone please be there — I want a great group shot for the yearbook.
Opening ceremony was incredible! The keynote speaker had everyone on their feet. So pumped for tomorrow.
Good luck to everyone competing today! 🚀

channel_5.text: Officer meeting recap: We confirmed all 47 members are registered for State. Hotel rooms are booked. Budget approved for $800 travel fund. Marcus is handling check-in table Day 1.
I'll have the check-in spreadsheet printed and ready. Name badges are being picked up tomorrow morning.
Social media is all set! I'll be posting live updates throughout the conference on the chapter Instagram. Send me your wins as they happen!
Officer meeting recap posted in resources 📋

channel_6.text: Hey all — sharing the scoring rubric for Business Ethics. PDF attached. Key focus areas: stakeholder analysis, ethical frameworks, case study application.

resource.title: Mobile App Dev — Judging Rubric & Scoring Sheet

channel_6.text: Anyone have good resources for Securities & Investments? I'm solid on stocks but need help with derivatives.
Investopedia's options guide is gold for derivatives. Also Khan Academy has a solid series on futures. DM me if you want my practice questions doc.
Sharing the Mobile App Dev guidelines and rubric here for anyone who needs it.

channel_7.text: NLC crew! Atlanta 2026, here we come. Let's make a plan. Who's qualified or expecting to qualify from State?
I qualified last year in Mobile App Dev and made top 10. This year I'm going back. The NLC experience is absolutely worth it — it changed my career direction.
I'm really hoping to qualify in Business Ethics. Top 3 go to NLC. It's competitive but I feel ready.
NLC registration opens April 10 — mark your calendars!

channel_8.text: Business Plan people — let's do one final pitch practice over video call tonight. Who's in? 8 PM?
In! My pitch is 6:45 — down from 8 minutes after cutting the market size section. Feels much tighter now.
Judges always ask about the financial projections. Make sure your Year 3 numbers are defensible. They grilled me on unit economics last year.
My executive summary is finally done 💪
